text: Been coming here since before I retired and it never gets old. Best crawfish on the Gulf Coast, period. The boil is seasoned just right -- not too hot, not too bland. They know how to do it down here. Friendly folks too. This is my go-to spot whenever the season is right. Already told everybody at church about this place.

Saturday morning tradition and I wouldn't have it any other way. Scattered, smothered, and covered hash browns, two eggs over easy, and a large OJ. The staff knows my order by now. Clean, fast, and exactly what you want on a weekend morning before heading out to the yard. This is real American food. Nothing fancy, just honest and good every single time.

Took my wife here for our anniversary and she was absolutely delighted. This place has history and you feel it the moment you walk in. The shrimp and grits were outstanding, and the bread pudding at the end -- Lord have mercy. Service was attentive without being pushy. Worth every penny for a special occasion. Been in Biloxi all my life and Mary Mahoney's still impresses me every time.

Good selection for woodworking supplies. Found the oak dowels and wood stain I needed without any trouble. The staff in the lumber section actually knew what they were talking about, which I appreciate. Prices are fair. Only reason I don't give five stars is the checkout lines can get long on weekends. Still my first stop for bigger projects.

Exactly the kind of bait shop this coast needs. Good live shrimp, fresh cut mullet, and the owner always knows what's biting and where. Picked up a new Carolina rig setup here last week and had a great afternoon out on the Back Bay. These folks are real fishermen serving real fishermen. Prices are honest and the coffee's not bad either.

Fresh as it gets. I picked up two pounds of shrimp and a flounder on Friday and had one of the best fish fries I've cooked in years. The flounder was so fresh it barely needed seasoning. If you live on the Coast and you're not buying your seafood here, you're missing out. Support local. This is where to go.
Only place I trust for a proper crawfish boil on the Gulf Coast. The corn and potatoes they throw in soak up all that seasoning and come out perfect every time.
Great crawfish but the wait on weekends is real. Get there early or call ahead. Once you sit down though, it's worth every minute.
Open 24 hours and the waffles are always perfectly crispy. Late-night crew is just as good as the morning crew. Classic Waffle House reliability.

A Biloxi institution. We came here for my parents' 40th anniversary and the whole experience was flawless. The courtyard is beautiful and the redfish was cooked to perfection. One of the best restaurants in Mississippi, full stop.
Historic building, excellent service, and the seafood gumbo is legendary. A bit pricey but you're paying for the atmosphere and the quality, and both deliver.
Big selection and the garden center in spring is excellent. Got all my raised bed lumber here. Staff in plumbing helped me figure out a tricky repair I'd been putting off for months.
Fascinating piece of Gulf Coast history. The house tour guide was very knowledgeable and the grounds are beautifully kept along the beach. Worth a couple hours on any weekend.
Launched my boat here three Saturdays in a row this spring. Ramp is clean, staff is helpful, and the parking is manageable if you get there by 6 AM. Beautiful spot with great access to the Sound.

This is the kind of hardware store they don't make anymore. The guy behind the counter actually walked me through exactly what screws I needed for my fence project. Way better service than any big box store.

Good quality hardwoods for woodworking projects. They had walnut and cherry in stock which is hard to find locally. Staff cut my pieces to rough dimension without charging extra. Will definitely be back.
Never disappoints. The sauce is what it's all about and they never skimp on it. Fast service even when the line looks long. A dependable lunch stop on the Pass.
Does the job for everyday shopping. The garden section in spring had decent plants. Checkout lines are long but that's every Walmart. Prices are hard to beat for staples.
The most welcoming congregation on the Coast. Pastor's sermons are grounded and real. The gospel choir alone is worth the drive. This community truly looks out for one another.

Best place on the Gulf Coast to get heat-tolerant plants and Gulf South gardening advice. The staff actually understand our climate and don't just hand you a plant that'll die in July. Got great okra starts and some beautiful crepe myrtles here.
Bought a dozen blue crabs here last weekend and they were incredibly fresh. The owner has been doing this for years and it shows. Cheapest and freshest seafood in Biloxi. Don't sleep on this place.
Real Texas-style fast food done right. The Whataburger with jalapeños hits every time. Drive-through moves surprisingly fast for how big the orders are. A solid choice on Pass Rd.

Came here for a birthday dinner and had a great time. The steakhouse inside is excellent and the room was comfortable. Casino floor is lively on weekends. Good option if you want a nice evening out on the coast.

If you need fresh local catch, this is the spot. They had speckled trout and redfish the morning I went. Prices are fair and the owner keeps a clean shop. Highly recommend to anyone cooking Gulf seafood at home.
Good value for feeding a family. The fried chicken and pot roast are consistently good. Dessert bar is a real treat. It gets crowded after church on Sunday but the staff keeps the lines moving.
Coffee on the porch with Skeeter this morning, watched the street wake up.
Cut the shelves for Janelle's garage today — oak for honesty.
Walked the raised beds with my coffee; tomatoes and okra coming in.
Building committee meets first Saturday, 8 AM — painting day in November.
Sunday table: collards, cornbread, smothered chicken, peach cobbler.
Called Harold; we'll see each other at the family gathering.
Curtis fished the bayou with me — more about the company than the catch.
Amara and Isaiah helped in the workshop, small tasks and big grins.
Gospel and blues on the workshop radio, Sam Cooke when the mood needs it.
Neighborhood loop walk at 6:30; the knees complain in the damp.
Grilled on the Weber this weekend, cheated on the sodium a little.
CPAP data review with Dr. Pham in November — hate the mask but wear it.
Built a gabled birdhouse, cedar for the smell, gave it away.
Doreen and I bickered about the thermostat, agreed on everything else.
Sun Herald over morning coffee, two cups black by 6.
Home Depot run for saw blades — brand-loyal and slow to buy.
Tithe to Shiloh Baptist; faith the quiet backbone of the week.
Braves on cable tonight, the Saints come Sunday.
Made Mama's gumbo — the weight of home in a pot.
Grandkids over for the weekend, garden and workshop lessons.
Roof's held fine since Zeta, AC newer, the truck runs clean.
Waffle House Saturday, the usual order.
Fixed the porch rocker; the rockers face the street on purpose.
Okra because Mama grew it, herbs by the back door.
